question: Check if three given side lengths `a`, `b`, and `c` can form a valid triangle. A triangle is valid if the sum of any two sides is greater than the third side.
errors: The function only checks one of the three triangle inequality conditions (a + b > c), instead of all three. For a valid triangle, all three conditions (a+b>c, a+c>b, b+c>a) must be true.
